title: Parallels-graph trio: commentarial load, relation-type stratigraphy, and coverage cartography
verdict: H0 (null wins on the one testable claim). Sub-study (C) is the only evaluable arm, and its lead negative control fails: the PLI-only intra-Pali sub-graph reproduces the same early-over-late coverage ordering the foreign graph shows, so the gradient reads as a SuttaCentral cataloguing-completeness artifact rather than differential cross-tradition transmission. Sub-studies (A) and (B) returned zero rows from a slug-prefix query defect and are untested non-results, not nulls supporting H0-A or H0-B.

analyst.density_finding: Only sub-study (C) is evaluable; (A) and (B) are non-results, and (C)'s decisive negative control fails, so the house thesis is not supported and the pre-committed null wins on the one testable claim. (C) external coverage: foreign coverage is 1early 0.3812 (1704/4470 passages) vs 2late 0.0620 (88/1420), and foreign edges-per-Mchar 444.882 (1early) vs 111.332 (2late). The raw early>late ordering predicted by H1-C is present and large (about 6x on coverage, 4x on density). BUT the lead negative control C2 (PLI-only intra-Pali sub-graph) reproduces the SAME early>late gradient at comparable or larger effect size: pli_coverage 1early 0.4025 (1799/4470) vs 2late 0.2415 (343/1420), and pli_edges-per-Mchar 762.357 (1early) vs 540.001 (2late). The intra-Pali control is not flat; it carries the identical direction (early>late) and in fact higher absolute coverage and density than the foreign graph in both strata. Per the protocol pre-registered falsifier ((C) FALSIFIED if the PLI-only control reproduces the early>late ordering at comparable effect size), H0-C is reported: the early>late coverage gradient is consistent with a SuttaCentral cataloguing-completeness artifact, not interpretable as differential cross-tradition transmission. The granularity bound C3 corroborates: SC distinct works fall 12 (1early) to 8 (2late) and SC rows 4470 to 1420, so the coverage denominator itself thins toward 2late, the exact cataloguing-density confound the control was built to catch. lang_breadth is flat at 3 in both strata, giving no breadth gradient to support H1-C. Sub-studies (A) and (B) cannot be adjudicated: A0/A1/A2 and B1/B2 all returned 0 rows because the collection filters work_slug LIKE 'dn%'/'mn%'/'sn%'/'an%' do not match the corpus slugs, which are prefixed pli-dn/pli-an etc, so no per-collection partial Spearman and no RetellShare exist to test against H1-A/H1-B. The register control A4 is not flat in raw mula density (NEG_REGISTER 104.718 hits/Mchar vs DHAMMA_EPITHET 3.194 vs COMM_METHOD attha 99.089) but with par_density never computed (A1 empty) there is no association to calibrate, so A4 cannot rescue or condemn (A). Net: H1-A and H1-B are untested non-results; H1-C is falsified by its own negative control and H0-C stands.
analyst.control_check: The lead negative control C2 (PLI-only intra-Pali coverage) did NOT stay flat: it moved in the same direction as the foreign signal and at comparable-or-larger magnitude. pli_coverage 0.4025 (1early) vs 0.2415 (2late) mirrors foreign_coverage 0.3812 vs 0.0620; pli_edges_per_Mchar 762.357 vs 540.001 mirrors foreign 444.882 vs 111.332. Because the intra-Pali control reproduces the early>late ordering, the protocol pre-registered falsifier triggers and the (C) effect is read as a SC cataloguing-completeness artifact rather than transmission history. C3 supports this: SC distinct works thin 12 (1early) to 8 (2late). The register control A4 (NEG_REGISTER 104.718/Mchar vs DHAMMA_EPITHET 3.194/Mchar) cannot serve as a control because the par_density axis it must calibrate against (A1) returned zero rows.
analyst.codebook: CODING IS BLOCKED AS DELIVERED; do not run it on this _raw.json. The protocol sets coding_needed=true and the rows live in CODE_pariyaya_sense_rows (ref column id, unique 200/200). The kwic column is unusable: the SQL requested substring .{0,80}(pariyay).{0,80} but every row captured only the bare token (189 rows = the lemma, 11 rows = null; max kwic length 7 chars), so there is zero surrounding context for a sense judgement. Blind coders cannot distinguish senses from a lone lemma; the sample must be re-pulled with real KWIC windows before coding. Once re-pulled, code each id into exactly one of: EXEGETICAL = pariyaya as commentarial method / synonym-gloss / word-analysis (alternative-term listing, vevacana-style equivalence, etymological unpacking); DISCOURSE-MODE = pariyaya as a manner/way/mode of teaching, an occasion, or a sequence/round (canonical narrative sense); OTHER-AMBIGUOUS = quotation fragment, formulaic list, or context insufficient to decide. Coders blind to stratum and work_role. Report Cohen kappa; require kappa >= 0.67 before any COMM_METHOD count is treated as a systematization signal, else revise the codebook on a fresh window-bearing sample and re-run. Re-pull note: current sample is 152 attha / 48 mula and skews 5comm (161 rows), so stratum-blinding and work_role-blinding are essential.

required_caveats: The early>late foreign-coverage gradient (C1) is reproduced by the PLI-only intra-Pali negative control (C2) at equal-or-larger effect size and is therefore a SuttaCentral cataloguing-completeness artifact, NOT differential cross-tradition transmission. The chronology/transmission reading is withdrawn per the pre-registered falsifier.

stratum is a work_slug lookup, not independent dating; no early/late claim here is chronological evidence. The 2late stratum is one-third the char-mass and distinct-work count of 1early (C3: 12 vs 8 SC works, 259.8M vs 76.5M chars), so its lower coverage denominator is a granularity gap, not an absence of parallels.

A second pre-registered C-falsifier also triggers: PLI (intra-Pali) coverage EXCEEDS foreign coverage in BOTH strata (0.4025 vs 0.3812 in 1early; 0.2415 vs 0.0620 in 2late). The graph is predominantly intra-Pali, so the cross-tradition-embedding frame is inapplicable and must not be asserted.

Sub-studies A and B returned zero rows because of a slug-prefix bug (filters use work_slug LIKE 'dn%'/'mn%'/'sn%'/'an%' but corpus slugs are pli-dn/pli-mn/pli-an/pli-sn, with the collection number living in the id column). H1-A and H1-B are UNTESTED, not falsified; they must NOT be reported as null support for H0-A/H0-B. A clean re-run requires fixing the filter (e.g. work_slug LIKE 'pli-dn%' or matching on id) before any A/B claim.

All per-character density figures in C1/C2 (foreign and PLI edges-per-Mchar: 444.882, 111.332, 762.357, 540.001) use a SUM(DISTINCT char_length) denominator that deduplicates equal-length passages and undercounts character mass non-uniformly across strata. These density numbers are uninterpretable as true per-character rates and must not be cited as magnitudes; only the coverage ratios (count(DISTINCT id) based) are sound.

The COMM_METHOD count (A3, 99.089/Mchar) cannot be treated as a systematization signal: the pariyaya sense-audit is unrun, and as delivered the CODE_pariyaya_sense_rows KWIC column captured only the bare lemma (no surrounding context), so the polysemy split (exegetical vs discourse-mode) is uncodeable. Re-pull with real .{0,80} windows, blind-code to stratum and work_role, and require Cohen kappa >= 0.67 before any COMM_METHOD interpretation. A3/A4 stay descriptive co-passes regardless.

Coverage absence is absence-of-cataloguing, not proven absence-of-parallel (parallel_have=false for foreign edges; the graph holds only edge/relation_type/lang, no foreign text). Findings are corpus-linguistic, not doctrinal or historical.
No em-dashes in deliverable prose. Report the publishable result as a NULL: the coverage gradient is a catalogue-density artifact; A and B are construction-blocked and pending a corrected re-run.

Correct the effect-size language: the PLI control C2 reproduces the early>late ORDERING (the pre-registered falsifier condition), but its gradient is SMALLER than the foreign gradient, not comparable-or-larger. Foreign coverage falls 6.15x (0.3812 to 0.0620) and density 4.0x; PLI falls only 1.67x (0.4025 to 0.2415) and density 1.41x. The control's HIGHER ABSOLUTE level in both strata is not effect size and must not be cited as if it were.

State explicitly that the steeper foreign decline is a real difference-in-differences residual (foreign declines about 1.88 log2 units more than PLI on coverage) and explain why it is NOT a salvageable H1-C transmission signal: no DiD rescue was pre-registered (the falsifier triggers on reproduced ordering alone); foreign parallels require active comparative cataloguing that SuttaCentral documents as incomplete for SN/AN, so the foreign edge type thinning faster where cataloguing is thinner is consistent with the confound; and flat lang_breadth (3/3) removes the axis that could separate thinner transmission from thinner cataloguing.

Cite the external corroboration as prior, published knowledge, not a novel finding: SuttaCentral states DN/MN parallels are essentially complete while SN/AN cataloguing has much research remaining, so differential cataloguing completeness by collection is the established baseline the C2 control was built to catch (C3 corroborates: SC distinct works 12 to 8, SC rows 4470 to 1420).

Report A and B as UNTESTED non-results from a query-construction defect (work_slug is prefixed pli-an/pli-dn so the LIKE 'dn%'/'mn%'/'sn%'/'an%' filters returned 0 rows), NOT as nulls supporting H0. No partial Spearman, RetellShare, or A4-vs-par_density correlation exists. A4 raw densities (NEG_REGISTER 104.718, DHAMMA_EPITHET 3.194) cannot adjudicate (A) because A1 is empty, so there is nothing to correlate against.

State that COMM_METHOD coding is blocked as delivered: the KWIC sample carries only the bare lemma (max 7 chars, 11/200 null) because the SQL substring(... from pattern) returned only the capture group, discarding the .{0,80} context. The A3 figure of 99.089 hits/Mchar must NOT be treated as a systematization signal until the sample is re-pulled with real windows and Cohen kappa >= 0.67 is met (current sample also skews 152 attha / 48 mula and 161/200 strat 5comm, so stratum and work_role blinding are essential).

Keep the claim corpus-linguistic: early/late is a work_slug lookup label, not independent dating, and the gradient is reported as cataloguing-completeness density, never as transmission chronology or a doctrinal verdict. Magnitudes are per-character (edges per Mchar) alongside coverage shares.

page_spec.abstract: Three structural questions over the SuttaCentral parallels graph were bundled into one study. Only sub-study (C), the early-versus-late coverage cartography, is evaluable. Its raw signal is large and in the predicted direction (foreign coverage 0.3812 early vs 0.0620 late), but the pre-registered lead negative control (the PLI-only intra-Pali sub-graph) reproduces the same early-over-late ordering (0.4025 vs 0.2415). The protocol fixed in advance that this falsifies the cross-tradition reading, so the null is reported: the gradient is consistent with a SuttaCentral cataloguing-completeness artifact, not differential transmission. Sub-studies (A) and (B) returned zero rows from a slug-prefix query defect and are untested non-results, not nulls.

sections.heading: The question and why it is a fair test
paragraphs: The house thesis is that the canon is supple while the commentary systematizes, and that heavily glossed suttas are also the most embedded in the cross-tradition parallel web. Each arm was given a pre-committed null and at least one negative control, so a positive reading could only stand if its control stayed flat.

Sub-study (C) was built to be decisive. Coverage, density, breadth, and relation-mix were measured per stratum on the foreign graph, then the same measurement was re-run on the intra-Pali graph as a control. If the early canon truly transmits across traditions more, the gradient should live in the foreign edges and not in the purely internal Pali edges. If both graphs thin together toward the late stratum, the economical explanation is incomplete cataloguing of the late material, and the gradient says nothing about transmission.

sections.heading: What the canon shows (sub-study C)
paragraphs: On the foreign graph the early-over-late ordering is present and large. Early coverage is 0.3812 (1704 of 4470 mula passages carry a foreign edge); late coverage is 0.0620 (88 of 1420). Foreign density is 444.882 edges per Mchar early against 111.332 late, roughly sixfold on coverage and fourfold on density.

Two measured quantities weaken the transmission reading before the control is consulted. Language breadth is flat at 3 in both strata, giving no breadth gradient to corroborate transmission. And the coverage denominator itself thins toward the late stratum: distinct SuttaCentral works fall from 12 to 8 and rows from 4470 to 1420, the exact cataloguing-density confound the control was built to catch.

sections.heading: The negative-control result (the falsifiable core)
paragraphs: The pre-registered decisive falsifier was that sub-study (C) fails if the PLI-only control reproduces the early-over-late ordering. It does. Intra-Pali coverage is 0.4025 early (1799 of 4470) against 0.2415 late (343 of 1420), and intra-Pali density is 762.357 edges per Mchar early against 540.001 late. The control did not stay flat; it moves in the same direction as the foreign signal in both strata.

The control is also higher in absolute level than the foreign graph in both strata (0.4025 over 0.3812 early; 0.2415 over 0.0620 late), tripping a second pre-registered falsifier: the graph is predominantly intra-Pali, so the cross-tradition-embedding frame is inapplicable. Absolute level is not effect size. The foreign decline is steeper (about 6.15x vs 1.67x on coverage), but that residual is not a rescue: no difference-in-differences salvage was pre-registered, the breadth axis is flat at 3 in both strata, and SuttaCentral documents its late-collection comparative cataloguing as incomplete. The pre-committed null H0-C stands.

Note on magnitudes: the edges-per-Mchar figures use a sum-of-distinct-char-length denominator that deduplicates equal-length passages unevenly across strata, so they are coarse magnitudes only. The coverage ratios, built on distinct-id counts, are the sound numbers.

table.caption: Sub-study (C): foreign graph vs its PLI-only intra-Pali negative control, mula layer. Both arms carry the early-over-late ordering, so the falsifier triggers.

sections.heading: Sub-studies A and B: untested, not null
paragraphs: The unit tables A0, A1, A2, B1, and B2 all returned 0 rows. The cause is a query-construction defect: the filters matched work_slug LIKE 'dn%'/'mn%'/'sn%'/'an%', but the corpus stores these collections under prefixed slugs such as pli-an and pli-sn, with the collection number in the id column (for example id an10.105 under work_slug pli-an). The C4 follow-up rows confirm this with 2798 rows under slugs like pli-an. No per-collection partial Spearman (A) and no RetellShare (B) were ever computed.
These arms are untested and must not be read as nulls for H0-A or H0-B. A clean re-run requires fixing the filter (match pli-dn% and the rest, or match on the id column) first.

The descriptive co-passes cannot rescue or condemn (A). The register control A4 is not flat in raw mula density (NEG_REGISTER 104.718 hits per Mchar, DHAMMA_EPITHET 3.194, COMM_METHOD in attha 99.089), but with par_density never computed there is no association to calibrate. The COMM_METHOD count cannot be a systematization signal because the pariyaya sense-audit is uncodeable as delivered: the KWIC column captured only the bare lemma (189 of 200 rows the token, 11 null), with no surrounding context. Re-pull with real windows, blind-code to stratum and work_role, and require Cohen kappa at or above 0.67 before any COMM_METHOD interpretation.

sections.heading: What it means and what it does NOT
paragraphs: Narrowly: on this corpus the early-versus-late external-coverage gradient is not interpretable as differential cross-tradition transmission, because its own intra-Pali control carries the same ordering. The cleanest reading is differential SuttaCentral cataloguing completeness by collection, corroborated by the thinning denominators in the late stratum.

It is not a chronological or transmission-history finding. Stratum is a work_slug lookup, not independent dating, so an early-versus-late disagreement is true by construction and never independent evidence. It is not a claim that the late canon has fewer real parallels, only fewer catalogued ones; an absent edge is absent cataloguing, not proven absent parallel, and the graph holds only edges, relation types, and languages, with no foreign text to verify. It is not a doctrinal verdict, and it says nothing for or against the house thesis in (A) and (B), which were never tested.

sections.heading: Auditability
paragraphs: Every count re-derives from two committed artifacts. Hypotheses, frozen lexica, the twelve enumeration queries, the negative controls, and the pre-registered falsifiers are in _protocol.json. Result rows are in _raw.json: C1 coverage and density, C2 control, C3 granularity bound, A3 COMM_METHOD density and A4 register densities, the empty A0/A1/A2/B1/B2 tables (rowcount 0), the 2798 C4 rows exposing the slug prefix, and the 200 pariyaya audit rows whose KWIC field shows the bare-lemma defect. Frozen denominators: mula 53.543921 Mchar, attha 29.488533 Mchar.

page_spec.verdict: H0. The one testable claim, sub-study (C), is falsified by its own pre-registered negative control, so the pre-committed null wins. Sub-studies (A) and (B) are construction-blocked untested non-results pending a corrected re-run.
scope_limits: Cannot establish chronology or transmission history: stratum() is a work_slug lookup, not independent dating; early/late is a label and any layer disagreement is true by construction, never independent evidence.

The early-over-late foreign-coverage gradient is reproduced by the PLI-only intra-Pali control at the same ordering, so it is read as a SuttaCentral cataloguing-completeness artifact, not differential transmission. The transmission reading is withdrawn per the pre-registered falsifier.

A second pre-registered falsifier also triggers: PLI coverage exceeds foreign coverage in both strata (0.4025 vs 0.3812 early; 0.2415 vs 0.0620 late). The graph is predominantly intra-Pali, so the cross-tradition-embedding frame is inapplicable.

Sub-studies (A) and (B) returned zero rows from a slug-prefix defect (filters used LIKE 'dn%'/'mn%'/'sn%'/'an%' but corpus slugs are pli-dn/pli-mn/pli-sn/pli-an, with the collection number in the id column). H1-A and H1-B are UNTESTED, not nulls; a corrected re-run is required before any A/B claim.

The edges-per-Mchar density figures (444.882, 111.332, 762.357, 540.001) use a SUM(DISTINCT char_length) denominator that deduplicates equal-length passages unevenly across strata; treat as coarse magnitudes only. The coverage ratios (distinct-id counts) are the sound numbers.

The COMM_METHOD count (A3, 99.089 per Mchar) is not a systematization signal: the pariyaya sense-audit is uncodeable as delivered (KWIC captured only the bare lemma, 11 of 200 null). Re-pull with real context windows, blind-code to stratum and work_role, and require Cohen kappa at or above 0.67 before interpretation. A3/A4 remain descriptive co-passes.

Coverage absence is absence-of-cataloguing, not proven absence-of-parallel; the graph holds only edges, relation types, and languages, no foreign text. Findings are corpus-linguistic, never doctrinal or historical.
